content.full_text:
October 6, 1992

Jill Stecher, Counsel  
Legal Division  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
428 J Street Suite 800  
P.O. Box 807  
Sacramento, California 95812

RE: REQUEST FOR ADVICE  
FILE NO. A-92-550

Dear Ms. Stecher:

In reviewing your advice letter with Councilmember Caballero, she asked that I provide further clarification regarding the second question posed in my letter dated August 4, 1992 in order to obtain additional advice.

Ms. Caballero and I understand the award of the maintenance contract may involve a conflict of interest depending on her economic interests vis-a-vis the person or business entity receiving the contract. The thrust of my question, however, was focused on the Council's preliminary decision whether or not to contract the work to outside contractors which must be decided prior to the Council receiving specific information about the particular contracting person or business entity.

In other words, does Ms. Caballero have a conflict of interest when determining the preliminary question of whether the work should be contracted through outside services or whether the City crew should do the work? The decision whether to have the maintenance work performed by City staff or by outside contractors could have an impact on the maintenance cost, but not necessarily, if the City and outside contractor costs are equal.

I hope that this correspondence adequately clarifies my second question. I am available to provide additional information, if needed, and I thank you for your continuing assistance and courtesies.

Sincerely,

[Signature]
STEPHANIE A. ATIGH  
CITY ATTORNEY

cc: Councilmember Anna Caballero

10/27/92  TC: Anna O'Weller

We discussed Q re: performance of maintenance work by city or outside contractor. The vote already occurred, she abstained. She explained that 2 districts mentioned in 1st letter are really 1 district. (She gave me facts re: decision, which C.A. never stated in either letter.)

I explained analysis - focus on foreseeability fact: no cost diff. between city or outside F doing the work. We discussed 18702.3 (c) & $10,000 effect on FMV. If no effect, she may participate.

Also discussed 18702.1 (a)(3)(c) - specific language re: fees assessed & that this applies to maint. dist. decisions re: annual fee, etc.

Explained our advice procedure. She is city, articulate, seems to understand but yet is contrary OK as crim. trial atty she doesn't want any complaints against her.

Each prop assessed annually
600 homes in dist → 2500 homes (growth potential)
